Q: I can see pinholes in the coating. What should I do?
A: Stop the line if you observe more than 5 pinholes per 100 m². Check the slurry for air bubbles — it
may need to be re-degassed. Inspect and clean the die lip for partial blockage. This corresponds to
alarm code COAT-ALM-005. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §6.
Q: How long can slurry wait before I coat with it?
A: Maximum 4 hours from slurry batch completion to start of coating. If the slurry is older than 4 hours,
re-measure viscosity before proceeding. If viscosity is OOS, do not coat — reject the slurry and notify
the Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §3, Step 1.
Q: What do I do if the oven temperature goes outside the alarm limit?
A: A deviation of ±5 °C from set point requires logging and monitoring. A deviation of ±10 °C requires
stopping the coating line. Do not advance foil through the oven. Check the heater and temperature
controller, and notify Maintenance if the fault persists for more than 10 minutes. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-COAT-002 §6, COAT-ALM-002.
Drying
Q: What is the maximum residual NMP allowed in a dried cathode electrode?
A: Less than 50 ppm NMP. If the inline IR sensor reads above 200 ppm, reduce line speed by 10% and
re-check. If residual NMP remains above 200 ppm after speed reduction, stop the line and inspect the
oven heaters. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003 §3.
Q: How long must cathode reels dry in the vacuum oven?
A: Minimum 12 hours at 120 °C with vacuum maintained below 10 Pa. Anode reels require a minimum
of 8 hours. Do not break the vacuum until the oven has cooled below 40 °C. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
DRY-003 §4.
Q: How long can a dried reel be exposed to ambient air before it must be used?
A: Maximum 30 minutes exposure to ambient air after the vacuum oven drying cycle. After that, the reel
must be stored in a dry room (RH < 1%) or nitrogen-purged storage. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003
§4, Step 6.
Calendaring
Q: The electrode coating is cracking at the rolls. What is the likely cause and what
should I do?
A: Cracking is most commonly caused by an under-dried electrode or excessive calendaring force.
Stop the line immediately. Reduce calendaring force by 10 kN and re-check. If cracking continues,
notify your Shift Supervisor — the reel may need to be returned for additional drying. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-CAL-004 §5, Step 4.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

results.question: What is the anode target solid content percentage?
results.llm_answer: The target solid content percentage for an anode electrode slurry is between 45% and 50%. If the measured solid content falls outside this range, the batch must be rejected. Do not attempt any adjustments.

20. Add SBR binder. Mix at LOW speed (200 RPM) for 20 minutes. Do NOT use high speed with

22. Proceed to Section 7 — Slurry Quality Check.

8. Slurry Transfer to Coating
23. Filter slurry through 100-mesh filter during transfer to coating vessel.
24. Record transfer volume and slurry batch ID on coating handover form.
25. Seal and label transfer vessel: Batch ID, slurry type, viscosity result, timestamp, operator ID.
26. Deliver to Coating Zone within 4 hours of batch completion. Record transfer timestamp.
27. Clean mixing vessel per Cleaning SOP-ELEC-CLEAN-003 immediately after transfer.
9. Alarms & Responses — Mixing Zone
Alarm Code Description Immediate Action
MIX-ALM-001 Mixer motor Stop mixer. Allow 15 min cool-down. Check for
overtemperature slurry hardening. If motor > 90 °C, call
Maintenance.
MIX-ALM-002 LEV airflow low STOP all solvent handling. Evacuate mixing area.
Do not re-enter until LEV restored. Notify
Maintenance and Supervisor.
MIX-ALM-003 Vessel vacuum loss Pause degassing. Check pump and seals. If
vacuum cannot be restored, degas with extended
low-speed mix (60 min). Record deviation.

results.question: Summarize the cathode slurry mixing procedure.
results.original_answer: The cathode slurry mixing procedure involves adding materials in a specific sequence: First, weigh PVDF binder and add to vessel. Add 60% of NMP solvent and mix at LOW speed (50 RPM) for 30 minutes. Add carbon black and mix at MEDIUM speed (500 RPM) for 45 minutes. Add 50% of NMC active material and mix at HIGH speed (1500 RPM) for 60 minutes. Add remaining 50% NMC and mix at HIGH speed for 60 minutes. Add remaining 40% NMP and mix at MEDIUM speed (800 RPM) for 20 minutes. Finally, apply vacuum (-0.08 MPa) for 30 minutes to degas while maintaining low-speed mixing.
results.llm_answer:
To mix cathode slurry:
1. Ensure LEV is operating, balance calibrated, and vessel clean.
2. Weigh PVDF binder and add to vessel. Add 60% of NMP solvent. Mix 30 min at low speed until PVDF dissolved.
3. Add carbon black. Mix 45 min at medium speed.
4. Add half of NMC active material. Mix 60 min at high speed.
5. Add remaining half of NMC. Mix 60 min at high speed.
6. Add remaining 40% NMP to target viscosity. Mix 20 min at medium speed.
7. Apply vacuum for 30 min to degas, keeping low speed mixing. 
8. Perform slurry quality checks before coating.

Operator FAQ — Electrode Manufacturing Knowledge Hub
SOP ID KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 Version v1.0
Process Area All Electrode Zones Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
This FAQ provides quick answers to common operator questions. All answers link to the authoritative
SOP. When in doubt, always refer to the full SOP.
Mixing
Q: What do I do if the slurry viscosity is too high after mixing?
A: Add small increments of solvent (NMP for cathode; deionised water for anode). Mix at medium
speed for 15 minutes after each addition, then re-measure viscosity. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts.
If still OOS after 2 attempts, reject the batch and notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
MIX-001 §7.
Q: Can I start mixing if ambient humidity is above 30% RH?
A: No. Mixing must not start if humidity exceeds 30% RH. NMP absorbs moisture which degrades slurry
quality. Log the environmental condition, notify your Shift Supervisor, and wait until humidity is within
range. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 8.
Q: The LEV green light is not lit. What do I do?
A: Do NOT open any solvent containers or start mixing. This is a P1 safety condition. Notify your Shift
Supervisor immediately and activate the Andon. Do not re-enter the mixing zone until LEV is confirmed
operational. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §3 and SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §2.
Q: How do I know if the mixing vessel is clean enough to use?
A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.

SOP ID SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 Version v2.1
Process Area Electrode Mixing Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 3
1. Purpose
This SOP defines the controlled procedure for preparing cathode and anode electrode slurries used in
lithium-ion battery manufacturing. Adherence is mandatory to ensure consistent slurry homogeneity,
correct viscosity, and repeatable coating performance.
2. Scope
Applicable to all Line Operators and Shift Supervisors assigned to the Mixing Zone. Covers batch
preparation from dry material weighing through final slurry quality check and transfer to coating.
3. Safety Requirements
Solvent vapours (NMP for cathode, water-based for anode) are present in the mixing
zone. Ensure LEV (Local Exhaust Ventilation) is active before opening any containers.
🦺 SAFETY
Wear chemical-resistant gloves, safety goggles, and lab coat at all times. NMP is a
reproductive hazard — pregnant workers must not enter the mixing zone.
• Verify LEV extraction is operating before starting — check green indicator on panel.
• Wear nitrile gloves (min. 0.15 mm), safety goggles, and chemical-resistant lab coat.
• Keep emergency eyewash station clear and functional.
• Solvent containers must be sealed when not in active use.
• No open flames or ignition sources within 5 m of mixing area.
4. Materials & Equipment
Material / Equipment Cathode (NMC) Anode (Graphite) Specification
Active Material NMC811 powder Artificial Graphite Per BOM – batch card
Binder PVDF (Kynar 761) CMC + SBR Battery grade
Solvent NMP (anhydrous) Deionised Water H₂O < 30 ppm (NMP)
Conductive Additive Carbon Black Carbon Black Per BOM
(C65) (C65)
Mixing Vessel Double planetary Double planetary Vessel clean, dry, certified
mixer mixer

Electrode Coating — Slot-Die Coating Process
SOP ID SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 Version v3.0
Process Area Electrode Coating Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 2
1. Purpose
Defines the operating procedure for slot-die coating of electrode slurry onto aluminium (cathode) and
copper (anode) current collector foil. Covers machine start-up, process parameter setting, in-process
monitoring, and shutdown.
2. Safety Requirements
NMP solvent vapour is present around the coating machine. Drying oven operates up
to 150 °C — risk of burns and solvent inhalation. PPE mandatory: heat-resistant
🦺 SAFETY
gloves near oven exits, chemical-resistant gloves near slurry, safety goggles. Confirm
oven door interlocks functional before start-up.
3. Pre-Coating Checks
28. Verify slurry batch ID and viscosity match coating work order. Slurry age must be < 4 hours. If >
4 hours, re-measure viscosity before proceeding.
29. Check foil reel: correct type (Al 16 µm ±1 µm for cathode; Cu 8 µm ±0.5 µm for anode), no
visible defects (wrinkles, holes, scratches).
30. Inspect slot die: clean lips, no dried slurry. Die gap set per recipe. Die bolts torqued to 8 N·m.
31. Prime slurry pump: run until no air bubbles in return line (minimum 2 minutes).
32. Set oven temperatures per recipe. Allow oven to reach set point and stabilise for minimum 15
minutes.
33. Run 0.5 m trial coat. Check coating weight and appearance before production run.
4. HMI Process Parameters — Coating
Parameter Cathode (NMC) Anode (Graphite) Alarm / Stop Limit
Coating Speed (m/min) 20–30 25–35 < 18 or > 38: stop line
Die Gap (µm) 150–200 180–220 ±15 µm from target: adjust
Wet Coating Weight (g/m²) 280–320 200–240 ±10%: stop, recheck die
Oven Zone 1 Temp (°C) 80 60 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop
Oven Zone 2 Temp (°C) 120 90 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop
Oven Zone 3 Temp (°C) 140 110 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop

Material / Equipment Cathode (NMC) Anode (Graphite) Specification
Viscometer Brookfield DV2T Brookfield DV2T Calibrated, within cert date
5. Pre-Mixing Checks
All pre-mixing checks must be completed and initialled on the Batch Record before
ℹ NOTE
proceeding. Do not begin weighing until all checks pass.
1. Confirm work order number and batch card match. Record Batch ID on the mixing log.
2. Verify mixing vessel is clean and dry. Check vessel certification tag — must be within 7-day
cleaning validity.
3. Confirm LEV is running: green LED on extraction panel is lit. Do NOT proceed if amber or red.
4. Check calibration status of balance (±0.1 g accuracy required). Confirm calibration sticker is
current.
5. Check calibration status of viscometer. Confirm spindle number matches recipe card.
6. Confirm active material moisture content < 0.1% (check drying oven log for last pre-dry run).
7. Confirm NMP moisture content < 30 ppm. Check certificate of analysis on drum.
8. Record ambient temperature and humidity. Temperature must be 20–25 °C; Humidity < 30%
RH. If out of range, notify Shift Supervisor before proceeding.
6. Mixing Procedure
6.1 Cathode Slurry (NMC)
Add materials in the exact sequence below. Out-of-sequence addition causes
⚡ CAUTION
agglomeration and will require the batch to be scrapped.
9. Weigh PVDF binder per batch card. Add to mixing vessel.
10. Add 60% of total NMP solvent. Mix at LOW speed (50 RPM) for 30 minutes until PVDF fully
dissolved.
11. Add carbon black (C65). Mix at MEDIUM speed (500 RPM) for 45 minutes.
12. Add 50% of NMC active material. Mix at HIGH speed (1500 RPM) for 60 minutes.
13. Add remaining 50% NMC. Mix at HIGH speed (1500 RPM) for 60 minutes.
14. Add remaining 40% NMP to adjust viscosity. Mix at MEDIUM speed (800 RPM) for 20 minutes.
15. Apply vacuum (−0.08 MPa) for 30 minutes to degas slurry. Maintain low-speed mixing during
degassing.
16. Proceed to Section 7 — Slurry Quality Check.
6.2 Anode Slurry (Graphite)
17. Dissolve CMC in deionised water (50% of total water). Mix at LOW speed (100 RPM) for 60
minutes.
18. Add carbon black. Mix at MEDIUM speed (500 RPM) for 30 minutes.
19. Add graphite active material in two equal portions, 30 minutes apart. Mix at HIGH speed (1200
RPM).

Operator FAQ — Electrode Manufacturing Knowledge Hub
SOP ID KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 Version v1.0
Process Area All Electrode Zones Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
This FAQ provides quick answers to common operator questions. All answers link to the authoritative
SOP. When in doubt, always refer to the full SOP.
Mixing
Q: What do I do if the slurry viscosity is too high after mixing?
A: Add small increments of solvent (NMP for cathode; deionised water for anode). Mix at medium
speed for 15 minutes after each addition, then re-measure viscosity. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts.
If still OOS after 2 attempts, reject the batch and notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
MIX-001 §7.
Q: Can I start mixing if ambient humidity is above 30% RH?
A: No. Mixing must not start if humidity exceeds 30% RH. NMP absorbs moisture which degrades slurry
quality. Log the environmental condition, notify your Shift Supervisor, and wait until humidity is within
range. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 8.
Q: The LEV green light is not lit. What do I do?
A: Do NOT open any solvent containers or start mixing. This is a P1 safety condition. Notify your Shift
Supervisor immediately and activate the Andon. Do not re-enter the mixing zone until LEV is confirmed
operational. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §3 and SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §2.
Q: How do I know if the mixing vessel is clean enough to use?
A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.

Scenario #3: Place the EV battery in a water dunk tank (either with or without salt)
When this may be appropriate: Through the creation of the emergency preparedness
plan, the business, the authority having jurisdiction (AHJ), and its insurance provider may
decide it would be appropriate to have a water dunk tank on the property ready for use.
The tank sits empty in a designated location where first responders can fill with water and
salt in correct proportions if desired. Salted water will extinguish the thermal runaway and
de-energize the battery more efficiently than just water alone.
Pros Cons
Safety of people and Will require significant amounts of water to be effective.
preservation of Battery may need to be further de-energized prior to shipping to
infrastructure. battery recycler if it was submerged in just water (vs. salted
Water is contained water).
and can be properly Risk of battery re-entering thermal runaway if it was submerged
remediated when the in just water (vs. salted water).
situation is over. If salted water is used there may be off gassing that needs
venting outdoors and/or a fire hose mist to redirect.
It can take multiple days to fully end the thermal runaway.
Proper disposal of water after event.
It is important to evaluate these methods within the context of an emergency preparedness
plan and considering such factors as the location, potential risks, available resources, and
environmental impact. Collaborating with the AHJ and relevant stakeholders can help
determine the most appropriate method for ending a thermal runaway while prioritizing
safety and minimizing potential damage.
De-energizing a Battery
De-energizing an EV battery prior to shipping may be appropriate in some situations where
the risk of a thermal runaway during transport is deemed high. The EV battery may have
been heavily damaged (dropped, struck, or involved in a vehicle accident), or had a
thermal runaway event. If a battery is considered damaged, defective or recalled (DDR),
refer to the manufacturer’s instructions regarding de-energization and potential risks.
Using a water dunk tank with the correct ratio of water to salt. Consider contacting a
professional organization with experience and the processes in place to manage a dunk
tank or establish those skills and processes within your organization with the input of trained
professionals.
Using an oscillator or other appropriate tool to de-energize the battery. Consider
contacting a professional organization with experience and the processes in place to use
an oscillator, load bank, resistor or other appropriate device or establish those skills and
processes within your organization with the input of trained professionals.
EV BATTERY SAFE HANDLING & STORAGE GUIDANCE | JULY 2023

results.question: Compare the coating speed ranges for cathode and anode electrodes.
results.original_answer: Cathode coating speed is 20–30 m/min, while anode coating speed is 25–35 m/min. Anode has a higher speed range.
results.llm_answer: The coating speed range for cathode electrodes is 20 to 30 meters per minute. For anode electrodes, the range is 25 to 35 meters per minute.

Electrode Drying — Inline & Vacuum Oven Process
SOP ID SOP-ELEC-DRY-003 Version v1.4
Process Area Electrode Drying Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
1. Purpose
Defines the controlled drying procedure to remove residual solvent from coated electrodes and achieve
required moisture levels for calendaring and cell assembly. Covers both inline drying and vacuum oven
batch drying.
2. Safety Requirements
Oven surfaces reach up to 150 °C. Thermal burns are a serious risk. Allow 30-minute
🦺 SAFETY cool-down before any maintenance access to oven zones. NMP vapour is extracted
via oven exhaust — verify exhaust fan is running before operating.
3. Inline Drying — Operating Checks
Inline drying occurs automatically within the coating machine oven. Zone temperature targets are set on
the coating HMI — see SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 Table 1.
34. Before starting, verify all three oven zones are at set point. Confirm exhaust flow indicator is
green.
35. Monitor NMP exhaust concentration meter during production. If reading approaches 25% LEL,
stop line and increase ventilation.
36. Check residual solvent in dried electrode every 1000 m using inline IR sensor. Target: < 50 ppm
NMP.
37. If residual NMP > 200 ppm: reduce line speed by 10% and re-check. If still > 200 ppm after
speed reduction, stop line and inspect oven heaters.
4. Vacuum Oven Batch Drying Procedure
Use for: (a) additional drying pass when inline drying fails spec, or (b) electrode reels awaiting coating >
24 hours after slurry application.
38. Pre-heat vacuum oven to 120 °C. Allow temperature to stabilise for 30 minutes.
39. Load electrode reels onto mandrel holders. Do not stack reels — each reel requires its own
holder.
40. Seal oven door. Draw vacuum to < 10 Pa. Record vacuum level at start.
41. Hold at 120 °C / < 10 Pa for minimum 12 hours (cathode) or 8 hours (anode).

Operator FAQ — Electrode Manufacturing Knowledge Hub
SOP ID KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 Version v1.0
Process Area All Electrode Zones Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
This FAQ provides quick answers to common operator questions. All answers link to the authoritative
SOP. When in doubt, always refer to the full SOP.
Mixing
Q: What do I do if the slurry viscosity is too high after mixing?
A: Add small increments of solvent (NMP for cathode; deionised water for anode). Mix at medium
speed for 15 minutes after each addition, then re-measure viscosity. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts.
If still OOS after 2 attempts, reject the batch and notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
MIX-001 §7.
Q: Can I start mixing if ambient humidity is above 30% RH?
A: No. Mixing must not start if humidity exceeds 30% RH. NMP absorbs moisture which degrades slurry
quality. Log the environmental condition, notify your Shift Supervisor, and wait until humidity is within
range. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 8.
Q: The LEV green light is not lit. What do I do?
A: Do NOT open any solvent containers or start mixing. This is a P1 safety condition. Notify your Shift
Supervisor immediately and activate the Andon. Do not re-enter the mixing zone until LEV is confirmed
operational. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §3 and SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §2.
Q: How do I know if the mixing vessel is clean enough to use?
A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

| SOP ID | KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 | Version | v1.0 |
| -------------- | ------------------- | ----------- | ------------------------ |
| Process Area | All Electrode Zones | Access Tier | Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3 |
| Effective Date | 11/03/2026 | Revision | Rev 1 |
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
General Safety
Q: What is the emergency stop (E-stop) procedure?
A: Press the nearest red mushroom-head E-stop button. This immediately de-energises the machine.
Shout 'E-STOP ACTIVATED' to alert nearby workers. Do NOT restart the machine after an E-stop
without first investigating and resolving the cause, and obtaining Shift Supervisor authorisation. Notify
the Shift Supervisor of every E-stop event.
Q: I can smell strong solvent but no alarm has triggered. What should I do?
A: Evacuate the area immediately and activate the nearest Andon. Do not wait for an alarm. Notify the
Shift Supervisor verbally. This may indicate a LEV failure or solvent spill before the sensor has detected
it. Re-entry only with Supervisor and EHS authorisation. Reference: SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §3, Step 3
— when in doubt, stop.
Q: If a quality hold is raised, who do I notify and how quickly?
A: Notify your Shift Supervisor and QA Lead within 15 minutes of raising the hold. Use MES hold
notification plus a verbal communication. Do not release a quality hold without QA Lead sign-off.
Reference: SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §4.
— END OF DOCUMENT — KB-ELEC-SOP-CORPUS-v1.0 — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY —
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

results.question: What happens if materials are added out of sequence during cathode slurry mixing?
results.original_answer: Out-of-sequence addition causes agglomeration and will require the batch to be scrapped.
results.llm_answer: If materials are added out of sequence during cathode slurry mixing, it will cause the active material particles to clump together (agglomeration). This ruins the slurry and the entire batch must be scrapped and cannot be used. Immediately stop mixing, clean the vessel thoroughly, record the incident, inform your Shift Supervisor, and prepare a new batch using the correct material sequence.
results.context:

Material / Equipment Cathode (NMC) Anode (Graphite) Specification
Viscometer Brookfield DV2T Brookfield DV2T Calibrated, within cert date
5. Pre-Mixing Checks
All pre-mixing checks must be completed and initialled on the Batch Record before
ℹ NOTE
proceeding. Do not begin weighing until all checks pass.
1. Confirm work order number and batch card match. Record Batch ID on the mixing log.
2. Verify mixing vessel is clean and dry. Check vessel certification tag — must be within 7-day
cleaning validity.
3. Confirm LEV is running: green LED on extraction panel is lit. Do NOT proceed if amber or red.
4. Check calibration status of balance (±0.1 g accuracy required). Confirm calibration sticker is
current.
5. Check calibration status of viscometer. Confirm spindle number matches recipe card.
6. Confirm active material moisture content < 0.1% (check drying oven log for last pre-dry run).
7. Confirm NMP moisture content < 30 ppm. Check certificate of analysis on drum.
8. Record ambient temperature and humidity. Temperature must be 20–25 °C; Humidity < 30%
RH. If out of range, notify Shift Supervisor before proceeding.
6. Mixing Procedure
6.1 Cathode Slurry (NMC)
Add materials in the exact sequence below. Out-of-sequence addition causes
⚡ CAUTION
agglomeration and will require the batch to be scrapped.
9. Weigh PVDF binder per batch card. Add to mixing vessel.
10. Add 60% of total NMP solvent. Mix at LOW speed (50 RPM) for 30 minutes until PVDF fully
dissolved.
11. Add carbon black (C65). Mix at MEDIUM speed (500 RPM) for 45 minutes.
12. Add 50% of NMC active material. Mix at HIGH speed (1500 RPM) for 60 minutes.
13. Add remaining 50% NMC. Mix at HIGH speed (1500 RPM) for 60 minutes.
14. Add remaining 40% NMP to adjust viscosity. Mix at MEDIUM speed (800 RPM) for 20 minutes.
15. Apply vacuum (−0.08 MPa) for 30 minutes to degas slurry. Maintain low-speed mixing during
degassing.
16. Proceed to Section 7 — Slurry Quality Check.
6.2 Anode Slurry (Graphite)
17. Dissolve CMC in deionised water (50% of total water). Mix at LOW speed (100 RPM) for 60
minutes.
18. Add carbon black. Mix at MEDIUM speed (500 RPM) for 30 minutes.
19. Add graphite active material in two equal portions, 30 minutes apart. Mix at HIGH speed (1200
RPM).

results.question: What is the difference in oven Zone 1 temperature between cathode and anode?
results.original_answer: Cathode requires 80°C while anode requires 60°C, a difference of 20°C.
results.llm_answer: For cathode electrode the oven Zone 1 temperature should be set to 80 °C. For anode electrode oven Zone 1 temperature should be set to 60 °C. Verify that the temperature reading stabilises within ±5 °C of the target before coating. If you observe a temperature deviation of more than ±10 °C from the set point, stop the line, log the event, and notify Shift Supervisor for investigation.
results.context:
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
Q: I can see pinholes in the coating. What should I do?
A: Stop the line if you observe more than 5 pinholes per 100 m². Check the slurry for air bubbles — it
may need to be re-degassed. Inspect and clean the die lip for partial blockage. This corresponds to
alarm code COAT-ALM-005. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §6.
Q: How long can slurry wait before I coat with it?
A: Maximum 4 hours from slurry batch completion to start of coating. If the slurry is older than 4 hours,
re-measure viscosity before proceeding. If viscosity is OOS, do not coat — reject the slurry and notify
the Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §3, Step 1.
Q: What do I do if the oven temperature goes outside the alarm limit?
A: A deviation of ±5 °C from set point requires logging and monitoring. A deviation of ±10 °C requires
stopping the coating line. Do not advance foil through the oven. Check the heater and temperature
controller, and notify Maintenance if the fault persists for more than 10 minutes. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-COAT-002 §6, COAT-ALM-002.
Drying
Q: What is the maximum residual NMP allowed in a dried cathode electrode?
A: Less than 50 ppm NMP. If the inline IR sensor reads above 200 ppm, reduce line speed by 10% and
re-check. If residual NMP remains above 200 ppm after speed reduction, stop the line and inspect the
oven heaters. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003 §3.
Q: How long must cathode reels dry in the vacuum oven?
A: Minimum 12 hours at 120 °C with vacuum maintained below 10 Pa. Anode reels require a minimum
of 8 hours. Do not break the vacuum until the oven has cooled below 40 °C. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
DRY-003 §4.
Q: How long can a dried reel be exposed to ambient air before it must be used?
A: Maximum 30 minutes exposure to ambient air after the vacuum oven drying cycle. After that, the reel
must be stored in a dry room (RH < 1%) or nitrogen-purged storage. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003
§4, Step 6.
Calendaring
Q: The electrode coating is cracking at the rolls. What is the likely cause and what
should I do?
A: Cracking is most commonly caused by an under-dried electrode or excessive calendaring force.
Stop the line immediately. Reduce calendaring force by 10 kN and re-check. If cracking continues,
notify your Shift Supervisor — the reel may need to be returned for additional drying. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-CAL-004 §5, Step 4.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

Electrode Coating — Slot-Die Coating Process
SOP ID SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 Version v3.0
Process Area Electrode Coating Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 2
1. Purpose
Defines the operating procedure for slot-die coating of electrode slurry onto aluminium (cathode) and
copper (anode) current collector foil. Covers machine start-up, process parameter setting, in-process
monitoring, and shutdown.
2. Safety Requirements
NMP solvent vapour is present around the coating machine. Drying oven operates up
to 150 °C — risk of burns and solvent inhalation. PPE mandatory: heat-resistant
🦺 SAFETY
gloves near oven exits, chemical-resistant gloves near slurry, safety goggles. Confirm
oven door interlocks functional before start-up.
3. Pre-Coating Checks
28. Verify slurry batch ID and viscosity match coating work order. Slurry age must be < 4 hours. If >
4 hours, re-measure viscosity before proceeding.
29. Check foil reel: correct type (Al 16 µm ±1 µm for cathode; Cu 8 µm ±0.5 µm for anode), no
visible defects (wrinkles, holes, scratches).
30. Inspect slot die: clean lips, no dried slurry. Die gap set per recipe. Die bolts torqued to 8 N·m.
31. Prime slurry pump: run until no air bubbles in return line (minimum 2 minutes).
32. Set oven temperatures per recipe. Allow oven to reach set point and stabilise for minimum 15
minutes.
33. Run 0.5 m trial coat. Check coating weight and appearance before production run.
4. HMI Process Parameters — Coating
Parameter Cathode (NMC) Anode (Graphite) Alarm / Stop Limit
Coating Speed (m/min) 20–30 25–35 < 18 or > 38: stop line
Die Gap (µm) 150–200 180–220 ±15 µm from target: adjust
Wet Coating Weight (g/m²) 280–320 200–240 ±10%: stop, recheck die
Oven Zone 1 Temp (°C) 80 60 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop
Oven Zone 2 Temp (°C) 120 90 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop
Oven Zone 3 Temp (°C) 140 110 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop

Electrode Calendaring — Density & Thickness Control
SOP ID SOP-ELEC-CAL-004 Version v2.2
Process Area Electrode Calendaring Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 2
1. Purpose
Covers the calendaring (compression rolling) process to compact dried electrode coating to target
density and thickness. Calendaring directly affects cell capacity, internal resistance, and electrolyte
wetting.
2. Safety Requirements
Calendaring rolls exert up to 200 kN of force. The roll nip point is a No-Reach zone
during operation. All guarding must be in place and interlocks active. No loose clothing
🦺 SAFETY
or jewellery near rolls. Use push sticks to guide foil — never use hands near nip
points.
3. Pre-Calendaring Checks
45. Verify electrode reel has passed drying quality check. Check reel tag for moisture result and
sign-off. Do NOT calendar undried or OOS moisture reels.
46. Set roll gap per recipe. Verify with feeler gauge on both operator side and drive side. Gap
difference must be < 2 µm.
47. Set roll temperature: Cathode 80 °C, Anode 25 °C (room temperature). Allow heated roll to
stabilise for 20 minutes.
48. Set line speed per recipe. Do not exceed 20 m/min until first 100 m quality check confirmed.
49. Thread leader foil through rolls to verify alignment before loading electrode reel.
4. Process Parameters — Calendaring
Parameter Cathode Target Anode Target OOS Action
Electrode Thickness (µm) 120–130 140–155 Stop, adjust roll gap.
Electrode Density (g/cm³) 3.3–3.5 1.5–1.6 Adjust gap; re-check.
Calendaring Force (kN) 100–180 60–100 Reduce if above maximum.
Roll Temperature (°C) 80 ± 5 25 ± 5 Stop if deviation > ±10 °C.
Line Speed (m/min) 10–20 15–25 Max 20 m/min until QC
confirmed.
Porosity (%) 30–35 38–42 QA hold; notify process
engineer.

results.question: What happens if LEV airflow is low (MIX-ALM-002)?
results.original_answer: STOP all solvent handling, evacuate mixing area, do not re-enter until LEV restored, and notify Maintenance and Supervisor.
results.llm_answer: STOP all solvent handling immediately. Evacuate the mixing area. Do NOT re-enter until LEV airflow is restored. Notify Maintenance and your Shift Supervisor. The line cannot continue until the ventilation issue is fixed.
results.context:
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
20. Add SBR binder. Mix at LOW speed (200 RPM) for 20 minutes. Do NOT use high speed with
SBR — shear degrades binder.
21. Adjust final viscosity with remaining deionised water. Mix at MEDIUM speed for 15 minutes.
22. Proceed to Section 7 — Slurry Quality Check.

8. Slurry Transfer to Coating
23. Filter slurry through 100-mesh filter during transfer to coating vessel.
24. Record transfer volume and slurry batch ID on coating handover form.
25. Seal and label transfer vessel: Batch ID, slurry type, viscosity result, timestamp, operator ID.
26. Deliver to Coating Zone within 4 hours of batch completion. Record transfer timestamp.
27. Clean mixing vessel per Cleaning SOP-ELEC-CLEAN-003 immediately after transfer.

Q: I can see pinholes in the coating. What should I do?
A: Stop the line if you observe more than 5 pinholes per 100 m². Check the slurry for air bubbles — it
may need to be re-degassed. Inspect and clean the die lip for partial blockage. This corresponds to
alarm code COAT-ALM-005. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §6.
Q: How long can slurry wait before I coat with it?
A: Maximum 4 hours from slurry batch completion to start of coating. If the slurry is older than 4 hours,
re-measure viscosity before proceeding. If viscosity is OOS, do not coat — reject the slurry and notify
the Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §3, Step 1.
Q: What do I do if the oven temperature goes outside the alarm limit?
A: A deviation of ±5 °C from set point requires logging and monitoring. A deviation of ±10 °C requires
stopping the coating line. Do not advance foil through the oven. Check the heater and temperature
controller, and notify Maintenance if the fault persists for more than 10 minutes. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-COAT-002 §6, COAT-ALM-002.
Drying
Q: What is the maximum residual NMP allowed in a dried cathode electrode?
A: Less than 50 ppm NMP. If the inline IR sensor reads above 200 ppm, reduce line speed by 10% and
re-check. If residual NMP remains above 200 ppm after speed reduction, stop the line and inspect the
oven heaters. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003 §3.
Q: How long must cathode reels dry in the vacuum oven?
A: Minimum 12 hours at 120 °C with vacuum maintained below 10 Pa. Anode reels require a minimum
of 8 hours. Do not break the vacuum until the oven has cooled below 40 °C. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
DRY-003 §4.
Q: How long can a dried reel be exposed to ambient air before it must be used?
A: Maximum 30 minutes exposure to ambient air after the vacuum oven drying cycle. After that, the reel
must be stored in a dry room (RH < 1%) or nitrogen-purged storage. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003
§4, Step 6.
Calendaring
Q: The electrode coating is cracking at the rolls. What is the likely cause and what
should I do?
A: Cracking is most commonly caused by an under-dried electrode or excessive calendaring force.
Stop the line immediately. Reduce calendaring force by 10 kN and re-check. If cracking continues,
notify your Shift Supervisor — the reel may need to be returned for additional drying. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-CAL-004 §5, Step 4.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
5. In-Process Checks
50. Every 200 m: Cut 100 cm² sample. Measure thickness at 5 points (4 corners + centre). All must
be within ±3 µm of target.
51. Every 500 m: Calculate electrode density from weight and thickness. Record on Calendaring
Log.
52. Continuous visual check: Watch for foil wrinkles, coating cracking, or delamination. Any of
these: stop line immediately.
53. If delaminating or cracking: reduce force by 10 kN and re-check. Continues → stop and notify
Shift Supervisor. Likely cause: under-dried electrode or excessive force.
6. Alarms — Calendaring
Alarm Code Description Response
CAL-ALM-001 Roll gap deviation > 5 µm Stop line. Re-zero gap sensor. Verify with feeler
gauge. Recalibrate if required.
CAL-ALM-002 Calendaring force > 200 kN Stop immediately. Reduce gap setting by 5 µm.
Re-thread and restart at low speed.
CAL-ALM-003 Roll temperature OOS > Stop foil advancement. Allow temperature to
±10 °C stabilise. If heater fault, call Maintenance.
CAL-ALM-004 Foil tension OOS Stop line. Check unwind and rewind tension
settings. Inspect foil for damage.

Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)
Battery Waste Management
1.Purpose: This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is meticulously designed to ensure effective battery
waste management in strict compliance with the Battery Waste Management Rules of 2022. The SOP
applies comprehensively to all units of THDCIL and encompasses the responsibilities and procedures
concerning the collection, segregation, transportation, and disposal of waste batteries. It also outlines the
prohibitions on heavy metal content in batteries.
2. Scope: This SOP is applicable comprehensively to all Projects/Units/Offices of THDCIL.
3. Definitions:
• Battery: Refers to new or refurbished cells and/or batteries and/or their components, including
accumulators, that generate electrical energy through the direct conversion of chemical energy. This
includes disposable primary and/or secondary batteries.
• Waste Battery: Encompasses various types of batteries, including used and/or end-of-life batteries,
pre-consumer off-spec batteries, batteries with expired usage dates, and batteries discarded by users.
4. Responsibility of Waste Generator (i.e. THDCIL):
It is the duty of consumer units/offices within THDCIL to:
• Discard waste batteries separately from other waste streams, especially mixed waste and domestic
waste.
• Ensure that waste batteries are disposed of in an environmentally friendly manner by giving them
to entities engaged in the collection, refurbishment, or recycling of batteries.
• Storage: THDCIL Projects/Units/Offices must store waste batteries in designated containers or
storage areas to prevent leakage and contamination. Separate storage facilities must be provided for
different types of batteries (e.g., primary and secondary batteries) and must comply with safety
regulations.
• Segregation: Waste batteries should be segregated based on their type, chemistry, and heavy metal
content. Separate containers labelled for different battery types (e.g., mercury-containing batteries)
should be used for proper identification.
• Transportation: Transportation of waste batteries should be done using specially designed and
covered transport systems to prevent foul odours, spillage, and environmental contamination.
• Disposal: Waste batteries should be disposed of through authorized entities engaged in battery
collection, refurbishment, or recycling. Ensure compliance with environmental regulations and
guidelines during disposal.

Operator FAQ — Electrode Manufacturing Knowledge Hub
SOP ID KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 Version v1.0
Process Area All Electrode Zones Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
This FAQ provides quick answers to common operator questions. All answers link to the authoritative
SOP. When in doubt, always refer to the full SOP.
Mixing
Q: What do I do if the slurry viscosity is too high after mixing?
A: Add small increments of solvent (NMP for cathode; deionised water for anode). Mix at medium
speed for 15 minutes after each addition, then re-measure viscosity. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts.
If still OOS after 2 attempts, reject the batch and notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
MIX-001 §7.
Q: Can I start mixing if ambient humidity is above 30% RH?
A: No. Mixing must not start if humidity exceeds 30% RH. NMP absorbs moisture which degrades slurry
quality. Log the environmental condition, notify your Shift Supervisor, and wait until humidity is within
range. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 8.
Q: The LEV green light is not lit. What do I do?
A: Do NOT open any solvent containers or start mixing. This is a P1 safety condition. Notify your Shift
Supervisor immediately and activate the Andon. Do not re-enter the mixing zone until LEV is confirmed
operational. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §3 and SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §2.
Q: How do I know if the mixing vessel is clean enough to use?
A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

results.question: What is the complete quality check procedure after mixing?
results.original_answer: After mixing (Page 3-4), perform slurry quality checks including viscosity (cathode: 4000-6000 mPa·s, anode: 2000-4000 mPa·s), solid content (cathode: 68-72%, anode: 45-50%), particle size D50 (cathode: 10-15 µm, anode: 15-20 µm), magnetic particle content (<0.1 ppm), and visual appearance (uniform, no lumps). Then transfer to coating (Page 4) within 4 hours.
results.llm_answer:
1. Verify vessel and tooling cleanliness using the certification tag.
2. Measure the wet weight using the calibrated balance: it must match the target weight ±5 g.
3. Assess slurry viscosity with the viscometer: target range is 1200-1300 mPa.s at 25°C. If OOS, adjust with solvent per SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §7.
4. Analyze particle size distribution using the laser diffraction system (LDS). Main peak (Δd32) must be 10-12 µm.

A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

| Process Area | All Electrode Zones | Access Tier | Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3 |
| Effective Date | 11/03/2026 | Revision | Rev 1 |
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
General Safety
Q: What is the emergency stop (E-stop) procedure?
A: Press the nearest red mushroom-head E-stop button. This immediately de-energises the machine.
Shout 'E-STOP ACTIVATED' to alert nearby workers. Do NOT restart the machine after an E-stop
without first investigating and resolving the cause, and obtaining Shift Supervisor authorisation. Notify
the Shift Supervisor of every E-stop event.
Q: I can smell strong solvent but no alarm has triggered. What should I do?
A: Evacuate the area immediately and activate the nearest Andon. Do not wait for an alarm. Notify the
Shift Supervisor verbally. This may indicate a LEV failure or solvent spill before the sensor has detected
it. Re-entry only with Supervisor and EHS authorisation. Reference: SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §3, Step 3
— when in doubt, stop.
Q: If a quality hold is raised, who do I notify and how quickly?
A: Notify your Shift Supervisor and QA Lead within 15 minutes of raising the hold. Use MES hold
notification plus a verbal communication. Do not release a quality hold without QA Lead sign-off.
Reference: SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §4.
— END OF DOCUMENT — KB-ELEC-SOP-CORPUS-v1.0 — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY —
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
Q: How do I verify the roll gap is equal on both sides?
A: Use a feeler gauge on both the operator side and the drive side of the roll gap. The difference
between the two sides must be less than 2 µm. If the difference is greater, adjust the drive-side screw
until both sides are within 2 µm of each other. Reference: SOP-ELEC-CAL-004 §3, Step 2.
Q: What do I do if the calendaring force exceeds 200 kN?
A: This is alarm CAL-ALM-002. Stop the line immediately. Reduce the roll gap setting by 5 µm. Re-
thread the foil and restart at low speed. Do not continue calendaring with force above 200 kN.
Reference: SOP-ELEC-CAL-004 §6.
Slitting
Q: When do I need to change the slitting blades?
A: Replace the blade set when cumulative cut length reaches 50,000 m, or immediately if edge burr
height exceeds 10 µm at any point during the shift. Check the blade log for current mileage at the start
of each shift. Reference: SOP-ELEC-SLIT-005 §3, Step 1.
Q: What should I do with a slit reel that has failed the width check?
A: Label the reel clearly as 'HOLD — QC REVIEW'. Move it to the designated hold area immediately.
Do not mix it with conforming reels. Record the reel ID, width measurement, and time on the Slitting
Log, then notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-SLIT-005 §6, Step 2.
Q: How soon must conforming slit reels be transferred to cell assembly?
A: Within 8 hours of completion, in sealed packaging. This prevents moisture uptake which could
degrade electrode performance. Reference: SOP-ELEC-SLIT-005 §6, Step 3.
Shift Handover
Q: Can I leave the zone before the incoming operator signs the handover record?
A: No. You must remain in the zone until the incoming operator has acknowledged the handover
verbally AND signed the MES Shift Handover Record. Leaving without a completed handover is a non-
conformance. Reference: SOP-ELEC-HANDOVER-006 §2.2, Step 4.
Q: What fields must I complete on the Shift Handover Record?
A: All fields are mandatory: Production Order Number, Current Reel/Batch ID, Production Output
(metres), Open Alarms (codes + status), Quality Holds (reel IDs + reason), Equipment Restrictions,
Consumable Status, Pending Actions, and both operator signatures. An incomplete handover record
must be flagged to the Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-HANDOVER-006 §4.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

Operator FAQ — Electrode Manufacturing Knowledge Hub
SOP ID KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 Version v1.0
Process Area All Electrode Zones Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
This FAQ provides quick answers to common operator questions. All answers link to the authoritative
SOP. When in doubt, always refer to the full SOP.
Mixing
Q: What do I do if the slurry viscosity is too high after mixing?
A: Add small increments of solvent (NMP for cathode; deionised water for anode). Mix at medium
speed for 15 minutes after each addition, then re-measure viscosity. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts.
If still OOS after 2 attempts, reject the batch and notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
MIX-001 §7.
Q: Can I start mixing if ambient humidity is above 30% RH?
A: No. Mixing must not start if humidity exceeds 30% RH. NMP absorbs moisture which degrades slurry
quality. Log the environmental condition, notify your Shift Supervisor, and wait until humidity is within
range. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 8.
Q: The LEV green light is not lit. What do I do?
A: Do NOT open any solvent containers or start mixing. This is a P1 safety condition. Notify your Shift
Supervisor immediately and activate the Andon. Do not re-enter the mixing zone until LEV is confirmed
operational. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §3 and SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §2.
Q: How do I know if the mixing vessel is clean enough to use?
A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

| Effective Date | 11/03/2026 | Revision | Rev 1 |
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
Q: I can see pinholes in the coating. What should I do?
A: Stop the line if you observe more than 5 pinholes per 100 m². Check the slurry for air bubbles — it
may need to be re-degassed. Inspect and clean the die lip for partial blockage. This corresponds to
alarm code COAT-ALM-005. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §6.
Q: How long can slurry wait before I coat with it?
A: Maximum 4 hours from slurry batch completion to start of coating. If the slurry is older than 4 hours,
re-measure viscosity before proceeding. If viscosity is OOS, do not coat — reject the slurry and notify
the Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §3, Step 1.
Q: What do I do if the oven temperature goes outside the alarm limit?
A: A deviation of ±5 °C from set point requires logging and monitoring. A deviation of ±10 °C requires
stopping the coating line. Do not advance foil through the oven. Check the heater and temperature
controller, and notify Maintenance if the fault persists for more than 10 minutes. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-COAT-002 §6, COAT-ALM-002.
Drying
Q: What is the maximum residual NMP allowed in a dried cathode electrode?
A: Less than 50 ppm NMP. If the inline IR sensor reads above 200 ppm, reduce line speed by 10% and
re-check. If residual NMP remains above 200 ppm after speed reduction, stop the line and inspect the
oven heaters. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003 §3.
Q: How long must cathode reels dry in the vacuum oven?
A: Minimum 12 hours at 120 °C with vacuum maintained below 10 Pa. Anode reels require a minimum
of 8 hours. Do not break the vacuum until the oven has cooled below 40 °C. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
DRY-003 §4.
Q: How long can a dried reel be exposed to ambient air before it must be used?
A: Maximum 30 minutes exposure to ambient air after the vacuum oven drying cycle. After that, the reel
must be stored in a dry room (RH < 1%) or nitrogen-purged storage. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003
§4, Step 6.
Calendaring
Q: The electrode coating is cracking at the rolls. What is the likely cause and what
should I do?
A: Cracking is most commonly caused by an under-dried electrode or excessive calendaring force.
Stop the line immediately. Reduce calendaring force by 10 kN and re-check. If cracking continues,
notify your Shift Supervisor — the reel may need to be returned for additional drying. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-CAL-004 §5, Step 4.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

Slitting

Shift Handover

Q: What fields must I complete on the Shift Handover Record?
A: All fields are mandatory: Production Order Number, Current Reel/Batch ID, Production Output

Consumable Status, Pending Actions, and both operator signatures. An incomplete handover record
must be flagged to the Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-HANDOVER-006 §4.

Electrode Coating — Slot-Die Coating Process
SOP ID SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 Version v3.0
Process Area Electrode Coating Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 2
1. Purpose
Defines the operating procedure for slot-die coating of electrode slurry onto aluminium (cathode) and
copper (anode) current collector foil. Covers machine start-up, process parameter setting, in-process
monitoring, and shutdown.
2. Safety Requirements
NMP solvent vapour is present around the coating machine. Drying oven operates up
to 150 °C — risk of burns and solvent inhalation. PPE mandatory: heat-resistant
🦺 SAFETY
gloves near oven exits, chemical-resistant gloves near slurry, safety goggles. Confirm
oven door interlocks functional before start-up.
3. Pre-Coating Checks
28. Verify slurry batch ID and viscosity match coating work order. Slurry age must be < 4 hours. If >
4 hours, re-measure viscosity before proceeding.
29. Check foil reel: correct type (Al 16 µm ±1 µm for cathode; Cu 8 µm ±0.5 µm for anode), no
visible defects (wrinkles, holes, scratches).
30. Inspect slot die: clean lips, no dried slurry. Die gap set per recipe. Die bolts torqued to 8 N·m.
31. Prime slurry pump: run until no air bubbles in return line (minimum 2 minutes).
32. Set oven temperatures per recipe. Allow oven to reach set point and stabilise for minimum 15
minutes.
33. Run 0.5 m trial coat. Check coating weight and appearance before production run.
4. HMI Process Parameters — Coating
Parameter Cathode (NMC) Anode (Graphite) Alarm / Stop Limit
Coating Speed (m/min) 20–30 25–35 < 18 or > 38: stop line
Die Gap (µm) 150–200 180–220 ±15 µm from target: adjust
Wet Coating Weight (g/m²) 280–320 200–240 ±10%: stop, recheck die
Oven Zone 1 Temp (°C) 80 60 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop
Oven Zone 2 Temp (°C) 120 90 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop
Oven Zone 3 Temp (°C) 140 110 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop

General Safety
Q: What is the emergency stop (E-stop) procedure?
A: Press the nearest red mushroom-head E-stop button. This immediately de-energises the machine.
Shout 'E-STOP ACTIVATED' to alert nearby workers. Do NOT restart the machine after an E-stop
without first investigating and resolving the cause, and obtaining Shift Supervisor authorisation. Notify
the Shift Supervisor of every E-stop event.
Q: I can smell strong solvent but no alarm has triggered. What should I do?
A: Evacuate the area immediately and activate the nearest Andon. Do not wait for an alarm. Notify the
Shift Supervisor verbally. This may indicate a LEV failure or solvent spill before the sensor has detected
it. Re-entry only with Supervisor and EHS authorisation. Reference: SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §3, Step 3
— when in doubt, stop.
Q: If a quality hold is raised, who do I notify and how quickly?
A: Notify your Shift Supervisor and QA Lead within 15 minutes of raising the hold. Use MES hold
notification plus a verbal communication. Do not release a quality hold without QA Lead sign-off.
Reference: SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §4.
— END OF DOCUMENT — KB-ELEC-SOP-CORPUS-v1.0 — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY —
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

results.question: What is the wet coating weight range for cathode?
results.llm_answer: The wet coating weight for cathode should be maintained between 280 g/m² and 320 g/m². If the reading falls outside this range, stop the line, inspect the die gap and pump pressure, make adjustments, and recheck the weight.

Operator FAQ — Electrode Manufacturing Knowledge Hub
SOP ID KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 Version v1.0
Process Area All Electrode Zones Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
This FAQ provides quick answers to common operator questions. All answers link to the authoritative
SOP. When in doubt, always refer to the full SOP.
Mixing
Q: What do I do if the slurry viscosity is too high after mixing?
A: Add small increments of solvent (NMP for cathode; deionised water for anode). Mix at medium
speed for 15 minutes after each addition, then re-measure viscosity. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts.
If still OOS after 2 attempts, reject the batch and notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
MIX-001 §7.
Q: Can I start mixing if ambient humidity is above 30% RH?
A: No. Mixing must not start if humidity exceeds 30% RH. NMP absorbs moisture which degrades slurry
quality. Log the environmental condition, notify your Shift Supervisor, and wait until humidity is within
range. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 8.
Q: The LEV green light is not lit. What do I do?
A: Do NOT open any solvent containers or start mixing. This is a P1 safety condition. Notify your Shift
Supervisor immediately and activate the Andon. Do not re-enter the mixing zone until LEV is confirmed
operational. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §3 and SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §2.
Q: How do I know if the mixing vessel is clean enough to use?
A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.

Electrode Coating — Slot-Die Coating Process
SOP ID SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 Version v3.0
Process Area Electrode Coating Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 2
1. Purpose
Defines the operating procedure for slot-die coating of electrode slurry onto aluminium (cathode) and
copper (anode) current collector foil. Covers machine start-up, process parameter setting, in-process
monitoring, and shutdown.
2. Safety Requirements
NMP solvent vapour is present around the coating machine. Drying oven operates up
to 150 °C — risk of burns and solvent inhalation. PPE mandatory: heat-resistant
🦺 SAFETY
gloves near oven exits, chemical-resistant gloves near slurry, safety goggles. Confirm
oven door interlocks functional before start-up.
3. Pre-Coating Checks
28. Verify slurry batch ID and viscosity match coating work order. Slurry age must be < 4 hours. If >
4 hours, re-measure viscosity before proceeding.
29. Check foil reel: correct type (Al 16 µm ±1 µm for cathode; Cu 8 µm ±0.5 µm for anode), no
visible defects (wrinkles, holes, scratches).
30. Inspect slot die: clean lips, no dried slurry. Die gap set per recipe. Die bolts torqued to 8 N·m.
31. Prime slurry pump: run until no air bubbles in return line (minimum 2 minutes).
32. Set oven temperatures per recipe. Allow oven to reach set point and stabilise for minimum 15
minutes.
33. Run 0.5 m trial coat. Check coating weight and appearance before production run.
4. HMI Process Parameters — Coating

Coating Speed (m/min) 20–30 25–35 < 18 or > 38: stop line

Oven Zone 2 Temp (°C) 120 90 ±5 °C: log; ±10 °C: stop

results.question: What is the alarm/stop limit for coating speed?
results.original_answer: < 18 or > 38: stop line
results.llm_answer: The stop limit for coating speed is < 18 m/min or > 38 m/min for either electrode type. If the coating speed goes outside this range, you must immediately press the E-stop to halt the line. Do not attempt to coat with out-of-spec speed. Verify the correct speed has been entered on the HMI, then restart the line once the speed is corrected.
results.context:
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
Q: I can see pinholes in the coating. What should I do?
A: Stop the line if you observe more than 5 pinholes per 100 m². Check the slurry for air bubbles — it
may need to be re-degassed. Inspect and clean the die lip for partial blockage. This corresponds to
alarm code COAT-ALM-005. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §6.
Q: How long can slurry wait before I coat with it?
A: Maximum 4 hours from slurry batch completion to start of coating. If the slurry is older than 4 hours,
re-measure viscosity before proceeding. If viscosity is OOS, do not coat — reject the slurry and notify
the Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §3, Step 1.
Q: What do I do if the oven temperature goes outside the alarm limit?
A: A deviation of ±5 °C from set point requires logging and monitoring. A deviation of ±10 °C requires
stopping the coating line. Do not advance foil through the oven. Check the heater and temperature
controller, and notify Maintenance if the fault persists for more than 10 minutes. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-COAT-002 §6, COAT-ALM-002.
Drying
Q: What is the maximum residual NMP allowed in a dried cathode electrode?
A: Less than 50 ppm NMP. If the inline IR sensor reads above 200 ppm, reduce line speed by 10% and
re-check. If residual NMP remains above 200 ppm after speed reduction, stop the line and inspect the
oven heaters. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003 §3.
Q: How long must cathode reels dry in the vacuum oven?
A: Minimum 12 hours at 120 °C with vacuum maintained below 10 Pa. Anode reels require a minimum
of 8 hours. Do not break the vacuum until the oven has cooled below 40 °C. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
DRY-003 §4.
Q: How long can a dried reel be exposed to ambient air before it must be used?
A: Maximum 30 minutes exposure to ambient air after the vacuum oven drying cycle. After that, the reel
must be stored in a dry room (RH < 1%) or nitrogen-purged storage. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003
§4, Step 6.
Calendaring
Q: The electrode coating is cracking at the rolls. What is the likely cause and what
should I do?
A: Cracking is most commonly caused by an under-dried electrode or excessive calendaring force.
Stop the line immediately. Reduce calendaring force by 10 kN and re-check. If cracking continues,
notify your Shift Supervisor — the reel may need to be returned for additional drying. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-CAL-004 §5, Step 4.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

results.question: What is the complete workflow from mixing to coating?
results.original_answer: Start with pre-mixing checks (Page 3), perform mixing procedure (Page 3-4), conduct slurry quality check (Page 4), filter slurry through 100-mesh filter during transfer, record transfer details, seal and label vessel, deliver to Coating Zone within 4 hours, and clean mixing vessel immediately after transfer.
results.llm_answer:
1. Check vessel certification tag: Verify cleaning date is within 7-day window. If tag missing/unclear, route vessel for cleaning before use.
2. Weigh dry electrode materials per BOM on certified balance: 
   - Cathode: NMC811 active material, PVDF binder, conductive carbon (C65), and solvent (NMP).
   - Anode: Graphite active material, CMC+ binder, conductive carbon (C65), and solvent (deionized water).
3. Add solvent (NMP for cathode, water for anode) to mixing vessel through solvent trap. Close trap securely.
4. Begin mixing in double planetary mixer at medium speed for 15 minutes. After mixing, verify slurry viscosity matches target (SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §7).
5. If viscosity is too high, add small solvent increments (refer SOP FAQ) and re-mix. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts allowed. Reject batch if still out of spec and notify Shift Supervisor.
6. Filter slurry through abrasion-resistant filter into a controlled environment (humidity <30% RH max).
7. Pass filtered slurry to coating zone via sealed transfer line. Do not transport slurry longer than 4 hours after mix completion. Verify slurry temperature within 20-25°C.
8. In coating zone, perform inline coating weight check every 200 m using beta gauge and manual patch sample. Apply slurry to foil and verify coating start conditions per SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.

the Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §3, Step 1.
Q: What do I do if the oven temperature goes outside the alarm limit?
A: A deviation of ±5 °C from set point requires logging and monitoring. A deviation of ±10 °C requires
stopping the coating line. Do not advance foil through the oven. Check the heater and temperature
controller, and notify Maintenance if the fault persists for more than 10 minutes. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-COAT-002 §6, COAT-ALM-002.
Drying
Q: What is the maximum residual NMP allowed in a dried cathode electrode?
A: Less than 50 ppm NMP. If the inline IR sensor reads above 200 ppm, reduce line speed by 10% and
re-check. If residual NMP remains above 200 ppm after speed reduction, stop the line and inspect the
oven heaters. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003 §3.
Q: How long must cathode reels dry in the vacuum oven?
A: Minimum 12 hours at 120 °C with vacuum maintained below 10 Pa. Anode reels require a minimum
of 8 hours. Do not break the vacuum until the oven has cooled below 40 °C. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
DRY-003 §4.
Q: How long can a dried reel be exposed to ambient air before it must be used?
A: Maximum 30 minutes exposure to ambient air after the vacuum oven drying cycle. After that, the reel
must be stored in a dry room (RH < 1%) or nitrogen-purged storage. Reference: SOP-ELEC-DRY-003
§4, Step 6.
Calendaring
Q: The electrode coating is cracking at the rolls. What is the likely cause and what
should I do?
A: Cracking is most commonly caused by an under-dried electrode or excessive calendaring force.
Stop the line immediately. Reduce calendaring force by 10 kN and re-check. If cracking continues,
notify your Shift Supervisor — the reel may need to be returned for additional drying. Reference: SOP-
ELEC-CAL-004 §5, Step 4.

Electrode Slurry Mixing — Cathode & Anode
SOP ID SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 Version v2.1
Process Area Electrode Mixing Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 3
1. Purpose
This SOP defines the controlled procedure for preparing cathode and anode electrode slurries used in
lithium-ion battery manufacturing. Adherence is mandatory to ensure consistent slurry homogeneity,
correct viscosity, and repeatable coating performance.
2. Scope
Applicable to all Line Operators and Shift Supervisors assigned to the Mixing Zone. Covers batch
preparation from dry material weighing through final slurry quality check and transfer to coating.
3. Safety Requirements
Solvent vapours (NMP for cathode, water-based for anode) are present in the mixing
zone. Ensure LEV (Local Exhaust Ventilation) is active before opening any containers.
🦺 SAFETY
Wear chemical-resistant gloves, safety goggles, and lab coat at all times. NMP is a
reproductive hazard — pregnant workers must not enter the mixing zone.
• Verify LEV extraction is operating before starting — check green indicator on panel.
• Wear nitrile gloves (min. 0.15 mm), safety goggles, and chemical-resistant lab coat.
• Keep emergency eyewash station clear and functional.
• Solvent containers must be sealed when not in active use.
• No open flames or ignition sources within 5 m of mixing area.
4. Materials & Equipment
Material / Equipment Cathode (NMC) Anode (Graphite) Specification
Active Material NMC811 powder Artificial Graphite Per BOM – batch card
Binder PVDF (Kynar 761) CMC + SBR Battery grade
Solvent NMP (anhydrous) Deionised Water H₂O < 30 ppm (NMP)
Conductive Additive Carbon Black Carbon Black Per BOM
(C65) (C65)
Mixing Vessel Double planetary Double planetary Vessel clean, dry, certified
mixer mixer

Electrode Drying — Inline & Vacuum Oven Process
SOP ID SOP-ELEC-DRY-003 Version v1.4
Process Area Electrode Drying Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
1. Purpose
Defines the controlled drying procedure to remove residual solvent from coated electrodes and achieve
required moisture levels for calendaring and cell assembly. Covers both inline drying and vacuum oven
batch drying.
2. Safety Requirements
Oven surfaces reach up to 150 °C. Thermal burns are a serious risk. Allow 30-minute
🦺 SAFETY cool-down before any maintenance access to oven zones. NMP vapour is extracted
via oven exhaust — verify exhaust fan is running before operating.
3. Inline Drying — Operating Checks
Inline drying occurs automatically within the coating machine oven. Zone temperature targets are set on
the coating HMI — see SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 Table 1.
34. Before starting, verify all three oven zones are at set point. Confirm exhaust flow indicator is
green.
35. Monitor NMP exhaust concentration meter during production. If reading approaches 25% LEL,
stop line and increase ventilation.
36. Check residual solvent in dried electrode every 1000 m using inline IR sensor. Target: < 50 ppm
NMP.
37. If residual NMP > 200 ppm: reduce line speed by 10% and re-check. If still > 200 ppm after
speed reduction, stop line and inspect oven heaters.
4. Vacuum Oven Batch Drying Procedure
Use for: (a) additional drying pass when inline drying fails spec, or (b) electrode reels awaiting coating >
24 hours after slurry application.
38. Pre-heat vacuum oven to 120 °C. Allow temperature to stabilise for 30 minutes.
39. Load electrode reels onto mandrel holders. Do not stack reels — each reel requires its own
holder.
40. Seal oven door. Draw vacuum to < 10 Pa. Record vacuum level at start.
41. Hold at 120 °C / < 10 Pa for minimum 12 hours (cathode) or 8 hours (anode).
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 v2.1 | Electrode Slurry MixingCONFIDENTIAL — AGRATAS GIGAFACTORY | INTERNAL USE ONLY
Operator FAQ — Electrode Manufacturing Knowledge Hub
SOP ID KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 Version v1.0
Process Area All Electrode Zones Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
This FAQ provides quick answers to common operator questions. All answers link to the authoritative
SOP. When in doubt, always refer to the full SOP.
Mixing
Q: What do I do if the slurry viscosity is too high after mixing?
A: Add small increments of solvent (NMP for cathode; deionised water for anode). Mix at medium
speed for 15 minutes after each addition, then re-measure viscosity. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts.
If still OOS after 2 attempts, reject the batch and notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
MIX-001 §7.
Q: Can I start mixing if ambient humidity is above 30% RH?
A: No. Mixing must not start if humidity exceeds 30% RH. NMP absorbs moisture which degrades slurry
quality. Log the environmental condition, notify your Shift Supervisor, and wait until humidity is within
range. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 8.
Q: The LEV green light is not lit. What do I do?
A: Do NOT open any solvent containers or start mixing. This is a P1 safety condition. Notify your Shift
Supervisor immediately and activate the Andon. Do not re-enter the mixing zone until LEV is confirmed
operational. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §3 and SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §2.
Q: How do I know if the mixing vessel is clean enough to use?
A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page

Operator FAQ — Electrode Manufacturing Knowledge Hub
SOP ID KB-ELEC-FAQ-001 Version v1.0
Process Area All Electrode Zones Access Tier Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3
Effective Date 11/03/2026 Revision Rev 1
This FAQ provides quick answers to common operator questions. All answers link to the authoritative
SOP. When in doubt, always refer to the full SOP.
Mixing
Q: What do I do if the slurry viscosity is too high after mixing?
A: Add small increments of solvent (NMP for cathode; deionised water for anode). Mix at medium
speed for 15 minutes after each addition, then re-measure viscosity. Maximum 2 adjustment attempts.
If still OOS after 2 attempts, reject the batch and notify your Shift Supervisor. Reference: SOP-ELEC-
MIX-001 §7.
Q: Can I start mixing if ambient humidity is above 30% RH?
A: No. Mixing must not start if humidity exceeds 30% RH. NMP absorbs moisture which degrades slurry
quality. Log the environmental condition, notify your Shift Supervisor, and wait until humidity is within
range. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 8.
Q: The LEV green light is not lit. What do I do?
A: Do NOT open any solvent containers or start mixing. This is a P1 safety condition. Notify your Shift
Supervisor immediately and activate the Andon. Do not re-enter the mixing zone until LEV is confirmed
operational. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §3 and SOP-ELEC-ALARM-007 §2.
Q: How do I know if the mixing vessel is clean enough to use?
A: Check the vessel certification tag on the vessel. The cleaning date must be within the 7-day cleaning
validity window. If the tag is absent, expired, or unclear, treat the vessel as unclean and route for
cleaning before use. Reference: SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 §5, Step 2.
Coating
Q: How often do I check coating weight during a production run?
A: Check wet coating weight every 200 m using the inline beta gauge plus a manual patch sample.
Check dry coating weight every 500 m by cutting a 100 cm² patch, weighing it, and subtracting the foil
weight. Record all results on the Coating Run Record. Reference: SOP-ELEC-COAT-002 §5.
SOP-ELEC-MIX-001 — Authorised Agratas personnel only. Page
